<!-- hive-mind:working-session-summary -->
## Working session summary

Implemented and pushed the issue 559 first-session planning deliverable.

PR updated: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/560  
Commit: `da5fa35f` on `issue-559-0655630e8231`

Added the case study under [docs/case-studies/issue-559/README.md](/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1782242171154/docs/case-studies/issue-559/README.md), including requirements, architecture inventory, solution plan, raw issue/PR research, online research, and the downloaded inspiration image. I left the PR as draft intentionally because the issue asks for planning approval before implementation.

Verification:
- `git diff --check`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- CI passed for `da5fa35f`: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/28051536425

Local `rust-script` checks could not run because `rust-script` is not installed here, but CI installed it and passed the workflow checks.

External sources used are cited in [online-research.md](/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1782242171154/docs/case-studies/issue-559/raw-data/online-research.md), including ReAct, Tree of Thoughts, Reflexion, Self-Refine, LangGraph, Microsoft Agent Framework, and AutoGen.

## ✅ Ready to merge

- All CI checks have passed
- No merge conflicts
- No pending changes

---
*Monitored by hive-mind with --auto-restart-until-mergeable flag*

Check https://voyager.minedojo.org and integrate it to our plan. Make sure our core problem solving algorithm is fully recursive. Meaning if we decompose recursively once we have small enough tasks they usually solvable as single line or even single call to methods from libraries.

For example https://voyager.minedojo.org (yet we should not try to use any neural networks) generates skills, have exploration tactics and strategies, critic tactics, skill building strategies, we instead can use Rust and other languages standard libraries as our core skills, we can also accumulate functions or skills as we go. Yet we also can search each term, each phrase, each sentence, each question online using all available search engines, for example google and others should be available via web search dependency in our Desktop app, so we can expand on search engines, rerank the results for each small peace of information, we can intelligently crawl all the the reranked results to collect relevant pieces of information, from all the collected we can find what needs more research, we can form hypothesis by checking found statements about the context in all the resources like docs, stackoverflow and other services.

We can go at both directions at the same time, we can try to understand from which pieces to construct our solution, or to which sub tasks and sub solutions to split the original task based on how it is usually split and so on.

Make our plan much more detailed and refined. Focus on missing pieces there are in our repository.

Make sure we stay true to https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-theory as much as we can (where everything is built from recursive, fractal unit of knowledge - a link). Where we don't use terms graph, and express both vertices and edges as links. So we can as in github.com/linksplatform and in https://web.archive.org/web/20181219134621/http://sentences.com/docs/amd.pdf focus on ommi competent programming and algorithms, generalization, algorithm as data, that is also translatable to code and back using our github.com/link-foundation/meta-language

So that all we have what we can construct from, we also have lots of issues in the past and some partial specialized solutions.

The end goal is to have true and full meta algorithm. The algorithm capable of modifying itself.

In links we can treat all thinks, objects, files, sequences everything as links, so most algorithms that are applied to some specific sub type of link or types in general, may be in fact applicable to wider range of things.

Also reread https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/559

Make our plan much more concrete, specific and more actionable, make it at least twice as detailed.

Also check all our dependencies from our organizations, and make sure all our components will have enough features, so if not enough we will pause execution of our plan until these features are delivered.

So apply to our plan all the thinks I told you right now, and also make sure all issues in all our upstream dependencies are created and listed in the GitHub comment, so I can focus on making sure we have exactly everything we need before next step.

If all dependencies already have everything we need, just make the plan itself with maximum detail possible.

🤖 **AI Work Session Started**
